title: National Guard and military authority questions
summary: Drop 22 focuses on military authority, National Guard activation, and constitutional emergency powers. Q frames the situation as a controlled operation while warning followers to prepare for major developments.
raw_text: Who controls the NG? Why was the NG recently activated in select cities within the US? Can the NG work in coordination w/ the marines? Do conditions need to be satisfied to authorize? What former President used the military to save the republic and what occurred exactly? Biggest drop to ever be provided on Pol. Study and prepare. The masses tend to panic in such situations. No war. No civil unrest. Clean and swift.

sources.label: Q Alerts archive
sources.note: Primary archive containing original Q drop text and timestamps

lines.text: Who controls the NG? Why was the NG recently activated in select cities within the US? Can the NG work in coordination w/ the marines? Do conditions need to be satisfied to authorize? What former President used the military to save the republic and what occurred exactly? Biggest drop to ever be prov
lines.fact: The drop was publicly posted to 4chan /pol/ on November 1, 2017 and archived by QAlerts and related archives. Several claims in the post are speculative and not independently verified.
lines.theory: Q community researchers interpreted this drop as evidence of a coordinated military intelligence operation occurring behind the scenes within the US government.
lines.context: The drops were posted during the first year of the Trump administration and shortly after public discussion surrounding sealed indictments, intelligence agencies, and political polarization.
lines.questions: What evidence independently confirms the operational claims? Are the references intentionally vague in ways that make retroactive interpretation possible? What specific evidence would falsify the implied narrative?
lines.Deltas: No directly verifiable yearly delta event conclusively tied to this drop was identified through mainstream archival review. Community interpretations frequently relied on post-hoc matching.
